quoteFirst: "Mr Furneaux has asked me--ordered me, in fact--to meet you by the side of the lake tomorrow morning at
quoteLast: and bring the drawings. Now you know why I have ventured to call this afternoon. I simply could not wait till I was brought before you like a collared thief with the loot in his possession. I _had_ to meet you without the intervention of a grinning policeman. When you heard my plea I thought, I hoped, that you might incline to a less severe view than would be possible if the matter came to your notice without warning."
